data.title: Rosenberg disputes Q3 GDP growth of 4.3% as he calculates real growth at 0.8%, citing government spending and depleted savings.
data.description: The U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis reported a 4.3% increase in third-quarter real GDP, but economist David Rosenberg argues the number is misleading. He believes the actual growth rate is around 0.8%, after accounting for factors like government spending and depleted savings. Despite official figures showing gains in consumer spending, exports, and government spending, Rosenberg claims these numbers are inflated. He points to stagnant personal disposable income growth as evidence that the economy is not as strong as it appears. Rosenberg alleges that the GDP report is manipulated, similar to the way CPI data is often questioned. This suggests that the economy may not be growing as robustly as recent data indicates.

data.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent confirms search for new Fed Chair to shrink institution, end perpetual QE era. Fed criticized as "engine of inequality".
data.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is actively interviewing candidates for the next Federal Reserve Chair, looking for a leader committed to reducing the central bank's influence and stopping perpetual quantitative easing. In a podcast, Bessent criticized the Fed's monetary policies of the past 15 years, particularly large-scale asset purchases that began in 2008. He called the Fed "the engine of inequality" for widening the wealth gap, stating it shouldn't exacerbate inequalities. Bessent argued that by keeping interest rates low and buying trillions in assets, the Fed benefited the wealthy while neglecting the general population. This shift in policy aims to create economic fairness by recalibrating the Fed's actions.

data.title: Sen. Warren warns Trump's Fed nominee will be a 'puppet,' cites Trump's loyalty vow. She raises alarm over president's intent for Fed Chair; warns of prioritizing loyalty over monetary independence.
data.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts is raising concerns in response to President Donald Trump's recent comments about his choice for the next Chair of the Federal Reserve after Jerome Powell steps down in May 2026. Trump's statement, made on Truth Social, stated, "Anybody that disagrees with me will never be the Fed Chairman," which Warren believes indicates a prioritization of loyalty over monetary independence in future nominees. She expressed that Trump's declaration makes it apparent that he intends to appoint a compliant individual who will cater to his own agenda rather than the interests of the American public. Warren emphasized the significance of this move and believes that the nominee will essentially be a "puppet" under Trump's control. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Treasury Sec Bessent states private economy booming under Trump, with GDP growth up 4.7%, contrast to Biden's led by govt spending.
data.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent recently remarked on the significant acceleration of the U.S. private economy during President Donald Trump's term, highlighting a robust growth in real economic output exclusive of government involvement. Specifically, Bessent emphasized that the private economy has been flourishing under Trump, with GDP growth, excluding government activity, surging by 4.7% at an annualized rate over the previous two quarters. He contrasted this with the administration under Biden, noting that previous growth was heavily reliant on government spending, leading to notable inflation and a decline in living standards. This positive trend in the private economy indicates a stark deviation from previous economic dynamics.

data.title: PowerBank's successful rocket launch deploys DeStarlink Genesis-1 satellite, advancing digital asset strategy in renewable energy and space-based computing.
data.description: The DeStarlink Genesis-1 satellite has been effectively launched and is currently operational in a low Earth orbit (LEO). This solar-powered infrastructure marks a significant progress in PowerBank's collaboration with Smartlink AI for their digital asset strategy. The satellite's deployment positions PowerBank at the intersection of renewable energy and space-based computing. This development has garnered attention post its successful operation, marking an important milestone in the collaboration.

data.title: Can-Fite BioPharma granted Brazilian patent for sexual dysfunction treatment using A3 adenosine receptor agonist.
data.description: Can-Fite BioPharma Ltd., a biotech firm, has received Patent No. BR112015002697-4 from the Brazilian Patent Office. This patent focuses on the use of an A3 Adenosine Receptor Agonist for treating Sexual Dysfunction. The company, listed on NYSE American as CANF and TASE as CANF, specializes in developing small-molecule drugs for oncological and inflammatory conditions. The announcement was made from Ramat Gan, Israel on December 26, 2025.

data.title: Crayola partners with McDonald's for global Happy Meal experience in 60+ countries, sparking kids' creativity worldwide.
data.description: In over 60 countries worldwide, a dynamic partnership has ignited children's creativity, turning ordinary family activities into fantastical adventures. Recently on December 26, 2025, Crayola and McDonald's joined forces for the inaugural global collaboration, introducing a fresh Happy Meal experience for kids. This unique venture is poised to revamp the way kids engage with their surroundings and spark imaginative pursuits.

data.title: Over the past year, memecoins have plunged by 65%, indicating waning interest and liquidity in the market.
data.description: Memecoins, which were once a measure of retail excitement in the market, are now ending the year with reduced liquidity, lower levels of participation, and diminishing speculative momentum.

data.title: Dow Jones and S&P 500 reach record highs before Christmas holiday, Palantir, Tesla, Google and Nvidia in buy zones.
data.description: The Dow Jones achieved a record high closing, while the S&P 500 reached a peak during the Christmas holiday period. Companies such as Palantir, Tesla, Google, and Nvidia are currently within recommended buying zones. After the holiday, Dow Jones Futures experienced a decline despite the overall stock market reaching new highs.
